description: Two pure functions listed in the Purity Boundary Analysis are not implemented: (1) has_body_changed(original: string, current: string) → boolean — behavioral-spec.md:102; (2) is_whitespace_only(body: string) → boolean — behavioral-spec.md:103. The verification-architecture.md assigns them IDs PURE-002 and PURE-003 respectively (lines 36-37). Neither function exists in editor.rs or anywhere else in the codebase. The spec's purity boundary analysis explicitly expects these as pure, side-effect-free functions that can be tested with unit tests without mocking. Their absence means the pure core is incompletely implemented, and any code that needs body-change detection or whitespace-only detection would either re-implement these predicates inline or rely on ad-hoc checks.

evidence.snippet:
// Only InMemoryNoteBody, NoteBodyStore, and validate_no_control_chars are defined.
// has_body_changed and is_whitespace_only are absent from the entire file (1612 lines).

expected: Implement pub fn has_body_changed(original: &str, current: &str) -> bool and pub fn is_whitespace_only(body: &str) -> bool as pure functions in editor.rs, with corresponding #[test] unit tests.
